message:
perf(server): skip redundant startup integrity probe and minimize readiness pool allocation

The integrity guard's background monitor was immediately repeating the
same integrity check that HTTP/TUI startup had just completed, wasting
~200-500ms on every server start.

- Add SKIP_NEXT_QUICK_CYCLE atomic flag and note_startup_integrity_probe_completed()
  so startup code can signal the guard to skip its first quick cycle
- Call the new function in both run_http() and run_http_with_tui() paths
- Reduce readiness check pool from auto-sized (potentially large on big hosts)
  to min=1/max=1 — readiness only needs one successful acquire to trigger
  migration initialization, and over-allocating causes unnecessary startup churn
- Remove unused tokio dependency from Cargo.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
